MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Against Plaintiff
Off Calendar Jun-24-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Matthew R. Lilligren, of Abbey, Weitzenberg, Warren & Emery PC, Tel. 707.542.5050, for plaintiffs;
Brendan M. Kunkle, of Abbey, Weitzenberg, Warren & Emery PC, Tel. 707.542.5050, for plaintiffs;
Rob Hulteng, of Littler Mendelson, P.C., Tel. 415.433.1940, for defendants;
Joseph M. Breall, of Breall & Breall, LLP, Tel. 415.345.0545, for cross-complainant.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AGAINST PLAINTIFF is denied because defendant failed to maintain its burden of production. The existence of an employment relationship is a factual issue. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding the existence of an employment relationship between Mr. Dawson and moving defendants and the factors highlighted in S.G. Borello & Sons, Inc. v. State Dept. of Indus Relations, (1989) 48 Cal.3d. 341, 350. Uber hired Dawson (Dawson depo., 46:2-6); Uber determined which drivers were offered which rides (Tolkin Depo., 85:17-23); Uber provided guidelines for when Dawson should be on duty (Tolkin Depo., 79:15-80:7; 100:9-103:12); Uber set ride acceptance standards, cancellation rates, and could terminate drivers (Tolkin Depo., 38:10-45:22); Dawson could not take hails when responding to an Uber call, ask for a tip, or solicit independent rides (Tolkin Depo., 106:8-10; 109:19-110:1); Uber tracked the rides, collected the fares, and paid Dawson a percentage (Dawson Depo., 81:19-82:17; Tolkin Depo., 58:24-61:22); and Uber had an "Onboarding Script" that controlled Dawson's conduct. Order signed in open court. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)

Desoto'S Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
Off Calendar May-14-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. BRENDAN M. KUNKLE OF ABBEY, WEITZENBERG & EMERY P.C., TEL. NO. (707) 542-5050 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JOSEPH M. BREALL OF BREALL & BREALL LLP, TEL. NO. 345-0545 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT DE SOTO LINE;
ATTY. RYAN B. POLK OF GORDON & REES LLP, TEL. NO. 986-5900 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS UBER & RASIER.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Desoto Cab Company, Inc's Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement - The court grants the motion for determination of a good faith settlement. See Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Assoc., (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488. "The party asserting the lack of good faith shall have the burden of proof on that issue." CCP 877.6(d). However, once the contestor attacks the settlement as lacking good faith, settlors are required to file counteraffidavits to make an evidentiary showing that the settlement was "in the ballpark." Settlors must provide substantial evidence to support a critical assumption as to the nature and extent of its liability. See Mattco Forge, Inc. v. Arthur Young & Co. (1995) 38 Cal.App.4th 1337, 1350, n. 6. In this case, the record as a whole (including Uber/Rasier's evidence) constitutes substantial evidence that the settlement is fair and reasonable. DeSoto is paying its policy limit on the only applicable insurance policy and plaintiff appears to be primarily responsible for the accident by jaywalking. Order signed in open court. Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Denise Doucette, CSR # 5963 =302/sb
